{"key_event":"Hung Assembly and protest-driven emergency governance","reasoning":"France retained a very strong administrative, coercive, and fiscal state, reinforced by COVID-era controls and security legislation, but loss of stable parliamentary majorities, budget crises, and fiscal strain reduced governing decisiveness. Civil society remained highly powerful: mass pension protests, farmers’ mobilization, electoral fragmentation, and opposition leverage constrained the executive, though polarization and emergency restrictions limited the increase.","society_change":0.2,"society_power":8.7,"state_change":-0.2,"state_power":8.6}
